# Siemens eHouse

Siemens eHouse delivers prefabricated electrical houses and modular power assemblies used to accelerate energization for industrial, utility-adjacent, and data-intensive sites.

## Why it matters
- Converts electrical rooms and packaged distribution into factory-built deployment units.
- Tightens the path from utility access or on-site generation into modular AI capacity delivery.
- Makes fast-track power availability feel operationally real rather than implied.

## Best fit
- Fast-track AI campuses that cannot wait for fully field-built electrical rooms.
- Modular or prefabricated deployments where energization speed matters.
- Projects evaluating packaged substations, ATS chains, and integrated power blocks together.

## Operational role in the graph
- Bridges `prefabricated-power-blocks`, `modular-substations`, and broader `power-and-electrical` decisions.
- Pulls `electrical-integration-services` and `infrastructure-logistics` into the same deployable path.
- Helps energy → packaged power → field execution become a believable site-delivery chain.
